request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. Each candidate is an extraction with candidate_id, knowledge_type, title, section_id, summary, evidence_quote, source_pages.

Acceptance criteria: Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

Allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

We must output JSON with verdicts array, each with candidate_id, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge (boolean), reason (short), and category_if_not (if rejected, specify one of those categories; if valid, null).

Let's evaluate each candidate:

1. cand_3b57dede4f2dfaee: Title "Zone Setpoint Overlap Prevention", section 5.3.2.4. Summary: Software shall prevent heating setpoint from exceeding cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F), unoccupied heating setpoint from exceeding occupied heating setpoint, and unoccupied cooling setpoint from being less than occupied cooling setpoint. Evidence quote: "5.3.2.4. The software shall prevent the following:" This is a clear operational rule about setpoints. It is grounded in the guideline. So valid.

2. cand_3bf792f851866da4: Title "Local Setpoint Adjustment Limits", section 5.3.2.5. Summary: Local setpoint adjustment offsets shall be persistent software points, limited by absolute limits: active occupied cooling setpoint between 22°C (72°F) and 27°C (80°F), active occupied heating setpoint between 18°C (65°F) and 22°C (72°F). Evidence quote: "5.3.2.5. Where the zone has a local setpoint adjustment knob/ button :" Likely valid operational knowledge. So valid.

3. cand_9f5b96d8af078c59: Title "Local Setpoint Adjustment Mode Restriction", section 5.3.2.5.d. Summary: Local setpoint adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied, Warmup, Cooldown modes; no impact in other modes. Evidence quote matches. Valid operational rule.

4. cand_54cd83216460ba6b: Title "Local Setpoint Adjustment Freeze During Demand Limiting", section 5.3.2.5.e. Summary: At onset of demand limiting, local set-point adjustment value shall be frozen, further adjustment suspended. Evidence quote matches. Valid.

5. cand_3d26e24e19f2743e: Title "Window Switch Setpoint Override", section 5.3.2.8. Summary: When window open, heating setpoint to 4°C (40°F), cooling to 49°C (120°F); if open during other than Occupied, Level 4 alarm. Evidence quote matches. Valid.

6. cand_25dd3ce7eef89884: Title "Local Override Timer", section 5.3.3. Summary: When thermostat override buttons depressed, call for Occupied Mode for 60 minutes. Evidence quote: "When thermostat override buttons are depressed, the call for Occupied Mode operation shall be sent to the Zone Group control for 60 minutes." This is operational. Valid.

7. cand_c491c8297b5d095d: Title "Heating Loop Enable/Disable", section 5.3.4.1.a. Summary: Heating Loop enabled when space temp below heating setpoint, disabled when above and loop output zero for 30 seconds. Evidence matches. Valid.

8. cand_e5f1ea12f2141d0d: Title "Cooling Loop Enable/Disable", section 5.3.4.1.b. Summary: Cooling Loop enabled when space temp above cooling setpoint, disabled when below and loop output zero for 30 seconds. Valid.

9. cand_15eab5047944f9c6: Title "Cooling Loop Output Range", section 5.3.4.2. Summary: Cooling Loop output 0% to 100%. Evidence matches. Valid.

10. cand_5eb654b78cf2b0e6: Title "Heating Loop Output Range", section 5.3.4.3. Summary: Heating Loop output 0% to 100%. Valid.

11. cand_6ac4ffd9f3068387: Title "Control Loop Type Requirement", section 5.3.4.4. Summary: Loops shall use proportional+integral logic; proportional-only not acceptable; integral gain small relative to proportional; P and I gains adjustable. Evidence matches. Valid operational requirement.

12. cand_82937d34036fd1c6: Title "Low Temperature Zone Alarm", section 5.3.6.1.b. Summary: Zone 2°C below heating setpoint for 10 min: Level 4 alarm; 3°C below for 10 min: Level 3 alarm. Evidence matches. Valid fault diagnostic rule.

13. cand_bfd549125a0ed615: Title "Active Setpoint Selection by Zone Group Mode", section 5.3.2.3. Summary: Active setpoints shall be occupied setpoints during occupied, warm-up, cooldown; unoccupied setpoints during unoccupied, setback, setup. Evidence matches. Valid.

14. cand_4c466240cddd8806: Title "Demand Limit Sliding Window Method", section "5.3.2.5 (informative note)". Summary: Demand control function shall use a sliding window method selectable in 1-min increments up to 60 min, 15-min default. Evidence quote matches. This is from an informative note. Informative notes are part of the guideline and provide operational guidance. It says "shall use", not "should", so it's a requirement. Likely valid as operational knowledge.

15. cand_6aa9a9aa3cb6abfc: Title "Demand Limit Level Reset Condition", section "5.3.2.5 (informative note)". Summary: Demand limit level switch set when measured demand exceeds limit; reset when demand <10% below limit for min 15 min and not within on-peak/partial-peak. Evidence quote matches. Valid operational rule.

16. cand_e612400cbed85894: Title "Single Setpoint Adjustment Moves Both Setpoints", section 5.3.2.5.c. Summary: If zone thermostat provides only single setpoint adjustment, adjustment moves both active heating and cooling setpoints up/down by same amount, within limits. Evidence quote matches. Valid.

All candidates appear to be valid, grounded operational knowledge from the guideline. No front matter, TOC, generic definitions, duplicates, or unsupported summaries. All summaries align with evidence quotes. So all are valid.

Thus, for each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge: true, reason: short, and category_if_not: null.

We'll produce JSON with verdicts array.
